title: Spiced winter simmer pot
subtitle: A stovetop simmering blend of cinnamon, cloves, star anise, and coffee for a warm seasonal fragrance
excerpt: A simmer pot is the simplest home fragrance method: whole spices simmered in water on the hob fill a room with scent within minutes. Cinnamon sticks, whole cloves, and star anise give a warm, spiced character; a teaspoon of instant coffee adds a dark, roasted note. Top up with water as needed and simmer for 1 to 2 hours. Not for consumption.
sourceNotes: Simmer pots (also called stove-top potpourri): a traditional home fragrance method. Aromatic compounds in spices (cinnamaldehyde in cinnamon, eugenol in cloves, anethole in star anise) are volatile and release readily in steam. The steam carries the scent molecules into the room air. Coffee contains hundreds of aromatic volatile compounds; instant coffee dissolves and releases them readily. Not suitable for consumption after simmering; discard after use. Monitor the pot and top up with water; never allow to boil dry.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The spice blend can be assembled in advance and stored in a jar. Add water and coffee fresh each time.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Make and use in one session. Discard after simmering. Do not consume. Do not leave unattended on the hob.

glossaryTerms.definition: An aromatic molecule that evaporates readily at low temperatures, carrying scent into the air. Spices such as cinnamon and cloves contain high concentrations of volatile aromatic compounds that are released when heated in water, which is why simmering spices fills a room with fragrance within minutes.

content.text: A simmer pot is the most immediate home fragrance method available: whole spices in water on the hob begin to fill a room with scent within a few minutes of reaching a gentle simmer. The

in cinnamon, cloves, and star anise evaporate with the steam and distribute through the air. Instant coffee adds a rich, roasted base note that deepens the spice blend and gives it a more complex character than spices alone. Top up with water as needed so the pan never dries out. Remove from the heat before leaving the room unattended. Do not consume the simmered liquid.

items.prepNote: Starting water; top up during simmering as needed
Broken in half to release more surface area
Adds a roasted base note; dissolves in the simmering water

content.text: Place the water in a small to medium saucepan. Add the cinnamon sticks (broken in half), cloves, star anise, and instant coffee. Bring to a gentle simmer over medium heat, then reduce to the lowest heat that keeps the water simmering. The scent will fill the room within 10 minutes. Top up with more water every 30 minutes or as needed to keep the pot at least a third full. Simmer for up to 2 hours.

Remove from the heat once done. Allow to cool. Discard the liquid and spices; do not consume. The whole spices can sometimes be used for a second simmer with reduced intensity; discard after two uses.
Make fresh for each session. The dry spice blend (without water) can be stored in a jar for several months and assembled quickly when needed. Always discard the simmered liquid after use.
